steps.title: Ensure Safety First
steps.content: Move away from traffic. Turn on hazard lights. Do not move injured people unless there is immediate danger (fire/flood).

steps.title: Call Emergency Services
steps.content: Call 112 (India) or local emergency number. State your location clearly. Stay on the line.

steps.title: Document the Scene
steps.content: Photograph vehicle positions, damage, and road conditions. Note time and location. Collect witness contact info.

steps.title: Basic First Aid
steps.content: Apply pressure to bleeding wounds. Do not give food or water to injured. Keep them warm and calm until help arrives.

steps.title: Assess the Situation
steps.content: Check if the person is conscious and breathing. Call 108 (Ambulance) immediately.

steps.title: CPR if Needed
steps.content: 30 chest compressions, 2 rescue breaths. Push hard and fast — 100-120 compressions per minute.

steps.title: Recovery Position
steps.content: If breathing but unconscious, place on their side to prevent choking. Monitor breathing continuously.

earthquake: Take COVER under desk or against interior wall
Stay away from windows and exterior walls
flood: Do not walk through flowing water
Turn off utilities at main switch
